reviews.review_msg: Chris is a very very good professor! I took CMPT 165 with him last semester. His PPT is useful and organized. He is also very helpful when you ask questions.  For midterm and final, I think they are pretty easy as long as you go through his PPT and exercises. I will definitely recommend him if you are going to take CMPT165 in SFU! Excellent!
This prof will help you and guide you through your obstacles. However those obstacles aren't particularly difficult.

LOVE HIM! Really nice, really helpful and always there to help you out. Always available through emails and office hours. Would answer hundreds of questions after class, cuz there would literally be a line of students rushing up to him and he was always so nice about it.

It's a really helpful course of basic HTMLCSS knowledge and skill. And Stephen is doing really well as a new teacher here. All HTMLCSS video casts are uploaded on Youtube which is extremely helpful. Python is a little bit tough, but he will give you many resources to guide you learn yourselves. Nice professor, excellent course, highly recommanded

He is a new teacher and doing extremely well in teaching.   He genuinely cares about his students and wants them to do well.   If you've got any questions about the course content, he will very willing to help. Sent him multiple emails asking for help, he replied to every single one of them in a lot of detail.

excellent prof, he's wants all his students to succeed. finishes lecture 10 mins early but uses those 10 mins as time to help students stuck on assignmentslabs. he's very clear on what is expected on the midtermfinals as long as you attend lecture and do well on the assignments.. this class will be a GPA booster ;)

He is helpful and nice to student. He will extend assignments and have passion towards teaching. Best professor ever in SFU, if you pay attention in lecture and can easily get minimum easy B or above. Highly recommended!!!!!
Nice guy! and easy take!
Is clearly a tech guy who never learned to teach. Poorly planned out course where he read off of slides and made sloppy mistakes
really easy going prof. Cheat sheets are allowed for midterms and final. he sometimes finishes lecture early but would stay for students to ask questions. very awesome class with him.

This course seems easy at first but it isn't unless you're in Comp. Sci.. Ms.  Tang is a bit unorganized and will often throw you off if this is your first time seeing the material. Homework and labs will be confusing but manageable. The midterm and final is scary with questions you've never seen before. I recommend CMPT120 as a prerequisite.
Poor lecturer. reads off powerpoint does not elaborate on anything. midterms are poorly written 0/10 would never take again
Never ever take her courseWorst prof ever
Very awful and unclear with her instructions
Worst teacher!! Knows nothing about computer science! Don't ever spoil your future taking class from this worthless teacher!!!

Definitely way too tough for an beginner-level CMPT course. If you decide to go along with it, be prepared to put in a lot effort or you won't get out alive. She doesn't allow cheat sheets for tests, while the other CMPT 165 profs do. For the 2 midterms we had, both averages were around 50% - and this class isn't scaled nor curved. Be warned.

Lisa taught the first portion (HTML) of the course very well, however, her lectures with Python were unclear and confusing. Exams and assignments are quite difficult, be prepared to work hard in this class if you do  not know anything about computer science at all.
Tough grader, some of the homework requires days of coding and troubleshooting, lectures are often repetitive as many people don't get the material, yet she just repeats original explanation.

She isn't good at teaching, I did go to almost all lectures but most of my learning was done at home. Her lectures are confusing, and she often like gets sarcastic or blames us when we don't understand certain concepts instead of trying to get us more interested. Like she'll often say "You guys should know this already, how don't you get this"

The class was full of kids who just played on their phone/laptop. Half the class never even shows up except for exams. Here's the real gist: it's a technical class. So put in the effort to study, Google (makes assignments easy) or ask TAs for help. Lectures aren't mandatory if you don't need hand-holding.
Lectures are unclear and confusing. She expects you to be able to do a lot of things in the assignments that she never taught. Make praying to the cmpt sci gods a ritual.
The best part of this course is that you will feel computer science is so funny to learn and easy to pass! I am so glad I can take my first CMPT 165 with this professor. Good luck guys.

Great Prof. Had him a year ago, don't know if he still teaches here. Notes crystal clear, simple and goes over things in lecture very well. Assignments easy but projects may need a little bit of effort. Marks & tests very fairly
Funny prof. But you need to know python well and prepare to code and debug. Best prof to be recommanded for CS students. May not be good if you don't know python.

Great prof! His work load is a bit much but nothing too bad. Definitely doable and worth doing since the concepts find their way in the quizzes and definitely the final exam. He's more than reasonable and always willing to help! His notes are amazing and he programs during lectures so the lectures are definitely worth attending! Overall, amazing!!!

Simply amazing! This guy is as close to the text-book definition of "perfect" as far as professors go. Passionate, helpful, reasonable and his notes are gold. I didn't have prior programming experience, but I still received an A+ in his class! Just study his notes, attend the lectures, ask for help, do his assignments, and you'll be fine. =]
AWESOME PROF! very clear. lecture notes are really useful. Reasonable tests.
Really cool, funny guy. This class was easy for me, since I had experience with the content already, but he's a great instructor.
this guy is amazing,he is nice and super helpful just go to his office hrs and you will be fine. his project is ok.. if you dont know how to do the things you can go through the ppt that he sent out.

I like him!!! He is a good teacher! But you need to work a little hard on his course..  He does give hard project, but he is a reasonable guy. The notes are there and he is helpful. If you try you will have fun time with him~ Do attend to his class, cuz he's class is funny.

Lectures clearly and in a straightforward manner. Kim is readily available outside of class and is extremely helpful when students have questions about how to do an assignment or lab. She also answers e-mails quickly and thoroughly.

Teacher doesnt really teach well to be honest. However he is very nice and kind but his teaching style and voice in lectures is difficult to understand. Doing practice exams and working hard is your best bet for this class.
Great prof. Very helpful outside of classes. Gives good feedback. For Cmpt 120 but it doesnt let me choose it
Distance ed - Never saw him ever. TA's had bad attitudes. Didn't respond to WEB CT and didn't even give us their SFU emails. Class is not for anyone who doesn't have programming background.
